summary: Matt Rocklin provides a development update from SciPy 2018, highlighting several Dask-related projects and discussions that emerged from the conference. The dask-jobqueue project for running Dask on HPC systems was particularly well-received, with configurations established for major supercomputers. Multiple integration efforts are underway including TPOT for automated machine learning, scikit-optimize for hyperparameter tuning, and UMAP for dimensionality reduction. The post also announces efforts to centralize PyData/Scipy tutorials on Binder and soft-launches Dask Stories for collecting user experiences.
key_points: The dask-jobqueue project enables Dask deployment on traditional HPC job schedulers (PBS, SGE, SLURM, etc.) and was a major hit at the conference
Multiple machine learning integrations are in progress: TPOT for automated ML, scikit-optimize for smarter hyperparameter search, and UMAP for dimensionality reduction
A new initiative aims to centralize PyData/Scipy tutorials on Binder with embedded YouTube videos to improve discoverability of educational content
Rocklin praises the competing Ray project and suggests Dask will adopt ideas for actors/stateful operations while hoping Ray adopts standard Python interfaces
Dask Stories was soft-launched to collect real-world usage stories from the community
money_quotes: These are very common among scientific, research, and high performance machine learning groups but commonly a bit hard to use with anything other than MPI.
I think that by composing with Binder Min effectively just created the free-to-use hosted Bokeh server service.
However, this content isn't very discoverable after the conference.
We're probably not getting as much value out of these resources as we could be.
I suspect that Dask will steal ideas for actors for stateful operation. I hope that Ray takes on ideas for using standard Python interfaces so that more of the community can adopt it more quickly.
Dask-ML is a young and fast-moving project with many opportunities for new developers to get involved.
Having people like yourself share how they use Dask is incredibly important for the project.
key_insight: Conference gatherings catalyze open source development not through completed work but by sparking collaborations, identifying integration opportunities, and building community around shared infrastructure challenges.
